childNodes.textContent:
Kitsoki mesa-sun favicon, inlined as a data URI: the SPA is built as a
         single go:embed'd file (vite-plugin-singlefile), so an external
         /favicon.svg reference would not be served. Source of truth:
         docs/branding/assets/mesa-sun-simple.svg (the small-size glyph).

Kitsoki "Mesa Sun" mark (docs/branding/logo.md, mesa-sun-simple.svg).
         Inlined as an SVG data URI so it survives vite-plugin-singlefile's
         single-file bundle and ships inside index.html — the Go web server
         (internal/runstatus/server) only routes "/" and "/rpc/*", so an
         external /favicon.svg would 404.

attributes._cssText: * { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0px; padding: 0px; }body { font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; background: var(--k-bg, #0f172a); color: var(--k-fg, #e2e8f0); }

Compact / collapsed: when the chat occupies a short space (e.g. the narrow
         sidebar surface where Chat shares height with Trace + Graph) the structured
         widgets won't fit, so collapse to a SINGLE-LINE text input. Free text always
         routes via session.turn (the semantic router / off-ramp), so this honors the
         text-only contract for every room. A disclosure icon reveals the hidden
         actions (and a count) when there are any; making the pane taller un-collapses
         automatically.

Choice items from typed view: labeled buttons with pre-filled slots.
         When present, these replace the legacy no-slot action buttons since
         choice items subsume them (back/look appear as choice items too).

attributes.title: back to the engineer's day landing

childNodes.textContent: back to the engineer's day landing
Param forms below (each needs a text input) — stacked vertically.

A wrapping, auto-growing textarea (NOT a single-line <input>): a
               long refine instruction typed into a single-line input scrolls its
               START off the left edge, so the operator's message "populates out
               of view" as they type — unreadable in a conversation and on camera.
               This wraps + grows so the full message is always visible. Enter
               still submits (Shift+Enter for a newline).

A wrapping, auto-growing textarea (NOT a single-line <input>): a
               long refine instruction typed into a single-line input scrolls its
               START off the left edge, so the operator's message "populates out
               of view" as they type — unreadable in a conversation and on camera.
               This wraps + grows so the full message is always visible. Enter
               still submits (Shift+Enter for a newline).

A wrapping, auto-growing textarea (NOT a single-line <input>): a
               long refine instruction typed into a single-line input scrolls its
               START off the left edge, so the operator's message "populates out
               of view" as they type — unreadable in a conversation and on camera.
               This wraps + grows so the full message is always visible. Enter
               still submits (Shift+Enter for a newline).

Free-text floor: a choice/form widget otherwise hides all free text,
         but the text-only contract (transports.md §7) says every room must be
         drivable by typing. This persistent, de-emphasized composer routes via
         session.turn (semantic router → agent off-ramp), so arbitrary text is
         always submittable alongside the structured widget — the only path to a
         no-match the off-ramp needs. Mirrors the TUI's Tab escape-hatch
         (choice_widget.go); the widget stays the primary affordance, this is the
         floor beneath it. Semantic/text-slot rooms already expose a text box, so
         they need no extra floor. rawDraft is shared with the semantic composer,
         so a draft survives any widget↔text switch.

View switcher. metro / ego / path are route-centric (they answer
           "where am I in the machine"); full is the whole static graph. Shown
           only when a current room resolves (see the mode watcher).

           design pipeline lives in one phase, so a phase spine would collapse
           it. Banners are declared metadata (parse.ts Room.banner).

Embed (VS Code): nothing beside the chat — Trace and Graph open as
             their own dockable windows via "Kitsoki: Open Trace" / "Open Graph".

Global meta-mode launcher + overlay. Mounted once at the shell so the
       overlay state (and its persistent chat) survives route navigation.

Global meta-mode launcher: a fixed button + a dropdown of modes. Hidden
       in snapshot/artifact mode (no live engine to chat with).

attributes.title: Meta mode — edit or ask about this story / kitsoki

childNodes.textContent:
Status badges: a meta chat is working (spinner) and/or has a reply
           waiting (dot). Both can show at once — distinct modes, distinct
           states.

Global guided-tour surface, same single-mount rationale: one tour can
       walk from the home screen into a live session without losing its place.

Persistent help launcher: replays the guided tour. Hidden in
       snapshot/artifact mode (no live server to drive). Sits bottom-right,
       stacked ABOVE the Meta launcher so the two never overlap.

Global inbox surface: badge launcher + panel + transient toast. Mounted
       once at the shell so the inbox follows the operator across every route.

Global inbox badge: a fixed launcher in the chrome, alongside the Meta
       button. Shows the unread count; turns the severity (action_required)
       color when any item needs attention, mirroring $inbox.needs_attention.

Global inbox panel: opens on badge click. A floating card anchored above
       the chrome launchers (bottom-right), reusing the Meta launcher placement
       rationale. Closes on backdrop click or Esc.

Transient toast on an SSE push (success / action_required only). Auto
       dismisses; click = jump to origin (same as a panel item).

Global forwarded-question surface: a hard modal shown when a dispatched
       agent forwards an AskUserQuestion into kitsoki and blocks for the answer.
Global bug-report review surface: capture → review → file.

node.textContent: prose / template: paragraphs with minimal inline-code rendering.
Say what you want — then launch
When you describe ad hoc work, the gate planner chooses a script gate, an agent-review gate, or a hybrid of both based on what would actually prove the work is done.
A Cherny loop iterates an agent toward a goal until a gate proves it met — or a budget stops it.
Say what you want in your own words.
_(not yet chosen — say what you want, or
(none — the maker works from the repo)
— no gate is locked in yet. Send a goal in your own words and the planner will choose whether this should be verified by a script, a prompt, or both.

childNodes.textContent:
Kitsoki mesa-sun favicon, inlined as a data URI: the SPA is built as a
         single go:embed'd file (vite-plugin-singlefile), so an external
         /favicon.svg reference would not be served. Source of truth:
         docs/branding/assets/mesa-sun-simple.svg (the small-size glyph).

Kitsoki "Mesa Sun" mark (docs/branding/logo.md, mesa-sun-simple.svg).
         Inlined as an SVG data URI so it survives vite-plugin-singlefile's
         single-file bundle and ships inside index.html — the Go web server
         (internal/runstatus/server) only routes "/" and "/rpc/*", so an
         external /favicon.svg would 404.

Running agent spend for this session. Always shown (not gated on
             `present`) so the operator can see the live cost ticking — which in a
             deterministic run is exactly the point: every state transition, guard,
             and git host call is free, so this reads $0.0000 until (and unless) an
             agent touchpoint fires. The :class flags the zero state for emphasis.

attributes.title: 0 agent call(s) · in 0 / out 0 tokens — deterministic transitions and git calls are free
Harness profile (backend/provider) — takes effect next turn
Model for the active profile — takes effect next turn
Reasoning effort — where the model supports it; takes effect next turn

childNodes.textContent:
Proposals badge: a count pill in the InteractiveView topbar, modelled on
       InboxBadge. Shows the number of queued mining/write-mode proposals; turns
       the attention (orange) colour when a write-mode opt-in is parked. Clicking
       it surfaces the head proposal in the SAME operator-question card the
       operator already answers (accept / refine / dismiss). Hidden when the
       queue is empty — the hide-when-zero contract the TUI badge also honours.
Staleness indicator + reload button for the toolbar

attributes.title: Story definition is up-to-date with the file on disk

childNodes.textContent: Reload warning: shown when the current state was removed by the edit.

Main row: chat (left) | trace (right).
           Browser: chat 46% | diagram+timeline 54%.
           Embed (VS Code): chat ONLY — trace + graph live in their own dockable
           windows (the "Kitsoki Surfaces" panels), so the chat panel never repeats
           them.

An off-ramp answer is a free-form converse reply that did NOT
             advance state (TurnResult mode "offpath"). The chip sits beside the
             "Agent" role so a viewer (and vision-QA) can tell it apart from a
             normal room transition and from a rejection — at a glance, in the
             same header line. The menu still shows because state is unchanged.

The turn's preserved thinking/tool feed, collapsed by default so
             the final view leads but the activity that produced it stays one
             click away (matching the live bubble it replaces).

Inline routing chip: how this free-text turn was resolved to an
             intent (tier + match reason + confidence). Surfaces the semantic-
             routing layer in the web chat the way the TUI does.
Streaming thinking bubble: visible while a turn is in flight

Compact / collapsed: when the chat occupies a short space (e.g. the narrow
         sidebar surface where Chat shares height with Trace + Graph) the structured
         widgets won't fit, so collapse to a SINGLE-LINE text input. Free text always
         routes via session.turn (the semantic router / off-ramp), so this honors the
         text-only contract for every room. A disclosure icon reveals the hidden
         actions (and a count) when there are any; making the pane taller un-collapses
         automatically.

Choice items from typed view: labeled buttons with pre-filled slots.
         When present, these replace the legacy no-slot action buttons since
         choice items subsume them (back/look appear as choice items too).
Plain buttons first (no Param) — rendered in a consistent grid.
✗ launch — say what you want first
Param forms below (each needs a text input) — stacked vertically.

Free-text floor: a choice/form widget otherwise hides all free text,
         but the text-only contract (transports.md §7) says every room must be
         drivable by typing. This persistent, de-emphasized composer routes via
         session.turn (semantic router → agent off-ramp), so arbitrary text is
         always submittable alongside the structured widget — the only path to a
         no-match the off-ramp needs. Mirrors the TUI's Tab escape-hatch
         (choice_widget.go); the widget stays the primary affordance, this is the
         floor beneath it. Semantic/text-slot rooms already expose a text box, so
         they need no extra floor. rawDraft is shared with the semantic composer,
         so a draft survives any widget↔text switch.

View switcher. metro / ego / path are route-centric (they answer
           "where am I in the machine"); full is the whole static graph. Shown
           only when a current room resolves (see the mode watcher).

attributes.title: Vertical route: traveled leg, current stop + live exits, road ahead
1-hop node-link: where you came from, where you are, where you can go
Breadcrumb of the traveled path + the current room's live exit chips
The whole static machine (every phase / room)

childNodes.textContent:
Three truth tiers, visually distinct per tools/runstatus/CLAUDE.md:
           traveled (TRACE, solid green), current (LIVE, amber) + horizon pills,
           road ahead (PROJECTION, muted/dashed). Stations are ROOMS — the
           design pipeline lives in one phase, so a phase spine would collapse
           it. Banners are declared metadata (parse.ts Room.banner).

Compact (docked panel): a one-line toggle so the filter bar doesn't crowd
         out the rows; collapsed by default.
Normal visit sub-header: one room visit = one accepted intent

Harness provenance: which profile/model answered this call.
                       Matches the operator's live picker selection.
Annotation badge: shown when at least one annotation targets this event's call_id.
Expanded body: effect groups get the world diff viewer; everything else uses EventDetail.

Embed (VS Code): nothing beside the chat — Trace and Graph open as
             their own dockable windows via "Kitsoki: Open Trace" / "Open Graph".

Global meta-mode launcher + overlay. Mounted once at the shell so the
       overlay state (and its persistent chat) survives route navigation.

Global meta-mode launcher: a fixed button + a dropdown of modes. Hidden
       in snapshot/artifact mode (no live engine to chat with).

attributes.title: Meta mode — edit or ask about this story / kitsoki

childNodes.textContent:
Status badges: a meta chat is working (spinner) and/or has a reply
           waiting (dot). Both can show at once — distinct modes, distinct
           states.

Global guided-tour surface, same single-mount rationale: one tour can
       walk from the home screen into a live session without losing its place.

Persistent help launcher: replays the guided tour. Hidden in
       snapshot/artifact mode (no live server to drive). Sits bottom-right,
       stacked ABOVE the Meta launcher so the two never overlap.

Global inbox surface: badge launcher + panel + transient toast. Mounted
       once at the shell so the inbox follows the operator across every route.

Global inbox badge: a fixed launcher in the chrome, alongside the Meta
       button. Shows the unread count; turns the severity (action_required)
       color when any item needs attention, mirroring $inbox.needs_attention.

Global inbox panel: opens on badge click. A floating card anchored above
       the chrome launchers (bottom-right), reusing the Meta launcher placement
       rationale. Closes on backdrop click or Esc.

Transient toast on an SSE push (success / action_required only). Auto
       dismisses; click = jump to origin (same as a panel item).

Global forwarded-question surface: a hard modal shown when a dispatched
       agent forwards an AskUserQuestion into kitsoki and blocks for the answer.
Global bug-report review surface: capture → review → file.
